danger_signals.visual_form: Sprite collision with hostile entities on overworld
danger_signals.meaning: Initiates battle encounter; signals immediate threat

danger_signals.visual_form: Hostile NPC sprites and faction-aligned enemies in towns and dungeons
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat to peaceful world-state; indicates disrupted regions

danger_signals.visual_form: Environmental changes in visited towns (Rococo and elsewhere affected by Hacker activity)
danger_signals.meaning: World-state degradation; peaceful areas under siege

safety_signals.visual_form: UI access to crafting/combination systems in safe locations
safety_signals.meaning: Player can prepare and strengthen robots; indicates peaceful town zones

safety_signals.visual_form: NPC character in Rococo representing paternal authority and inventor legacy
safety_signals.meaning: Stable anchor point; source of guidance and legitimacy

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Hostile sprite variants distinguishing Hacker-aligned enemies from neutral creatures
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Corrupted or disrupted town areas; presence in multiple zones indicating organized uprising
Rococo as operational base; inventor shops and safe zones; peaceful town infrastructure

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Planet Quintenix portrayed as stable; towns functional with normal NPC activity
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs engaged in normal routines; no hostile encounters in core areas

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Towns visibly disrupted (Rococo and elsewhere); Hacker forces present; environmental decay
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased hostile encounters; faction-aligned enemies in previously safe areas; changed NPC dialogue/behavior

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Corruption spreads to multiple zones; Gateau's space fortress becomes accessible/visible as final threat
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy difficulty increases; Hacker presence intensifies; narrative tension rises toward endgame

environmental_storytelling.example: The Hackers' influence visible through their presence in multiple towns and regions; disrupted areas show faction occupation
Tetron shards scattered throughout Quintenix; discovering fragments reveals ancestor Rask's history and Gateau's ambition
Dr. Akihabara's refusal to join Hackers establishes moral geography; protagonist inherits father's values through narrative positioning
Hackers' initial local uprising in Rococo escalates to planetary scope, culminating in space fortress; world-state deteriorates visibly across regions

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Organized disruption of towns; seeking Dr. Akihabara's recruitment; pursuing Tetron fragments
Operating from space fortress; attempting universal control via Tetron's time-manipulation properties
Refuses Hacker recruitment; settles in Rococo; provides guidance to protagonist; represents resistance anchor
Enter battle on map contact; can be switched between (with turn cost); move freely on battlefield during player turn
Drop items/equipment on defeat; can be encountered for experience/resource farming; recycled for currency

notes: Robotrek uses a Pokémon-like monster-raising paradigm (raising 3 customizable robots) to communicate world-state through NPC allegiances and enemy faction positioning. Danger is signaled through enemy contact and Hacker presence in formerly peaceful towns. Safety manifests through access to invention/crafting systems and paternal authority figures. The political conflict (Hackers vs. peaceful society) is communicated primarily through narrative and NPC behavior rather than explicit visual color-coding or sigils. World-state degradation progresses spatially as The Hackers' influence spreads from Rococo outward. The endgame threat (Gateau's space fortress and Tetron control) represents escalation from local uprising to existential/universal-scale danger.
